limit.description: Maximum concurrent calls. Null for system routes.

gateway.description: Gateway IP:port or system route name (USER, T30, etc.)

system.description: True for built-in system route types.

callingName.example: Caller ID display name; 15 characters or fewer

cnam.description: Outbound caller name. Max 15 alphanumeric characters.

customerOrderReference.description: Optional order reference. Auto-generated if omitted.

end.description: End time as Unix timestamp. Defaults to 24 hours before current time.

start.description: Start time as Unix timestamp. Defaults to current time.

customerOrderReference.description: Optional. If omitted, the proxy auto-generates a reference as {username}-{timestamp}.

number.description: The number whose LIDB was updated.

cnam.description: The sanitised caller name written to local cache and submitted to the carrier (max 15 alphanumeric).

customerOrderReference.description: Order reference used when posting to the carrier. Echoed from the request if supplied; auto-generated as '<username>-<unix-timestamp>' otherwise.

carrierStatus.description: Status string returned by the upstream LIDB carrier. 'Success' indicates the carrier accepted the request; other values describe the carrier-side failure. Local cache is updated regardless of this field.

cidr.description: IPv4 CIDR. Must be routable public address. Mask must be /8, /16, /24, or /32.

authDate.description: Auto-set to 2 days ago; callers do not need to provide this field.

e911Entry.description: e911 provisioning record. The sms field is always removed. Additional fields may be present.

paid.description: Invoice fulfillment status. true = customer has paid the invoice for this credit. false = invoice is still outstanding.

date.description: Timestamp the credit was applied to the account.

credits.description: Credits with outstanding invoices (paid=false), newest first.

streetType.example: End user address street type (e.g. St, Ave)

locationType3.example: End user address location type 3 (e.g. Flr, Ste)

typeOfService.example: Acceptable values are B and R (business and residence)

locationType2.example: End user address location type 2 (e.g. Flr, Ste)

locationType1.example: End user address location type 1 (e.g. Bld, Apt)

streetPreDir.example: End user address street direction prefix (e.g. N)

streetPostDir.example: End user address street direction ending (e.g. SW)

type.description: email=forward to email address, webhook=HTTP POST, sip=direct IP delivery.

resource.description: Email address, webhook URL, or IP address depending on type.

paid.description: Invoice fulfillment status. Always true on the /credits/paid endpoint. Always false on the /credits/unpaid endpoint.

date.description: Timestamp the credit was applied to the account.

enabled.description: true to enable inbound CNAM lookup, false to disable.

payments.description: All PayPal payments for the account, newest first.

password.description: 6-10 alphanumeric characters with at least one letter and one number.

credits.description: Credits with fulfilled invoices (paid=true), newest first.

authType.description: 0=Digest, 1=IP Auth, 2=Digest OR IP Auth, 3=Digest AND IP Auth

prefix.description: Digits to prepend on outbound calls.

gateway.description: IP or hostname with optional port. Must resolve to a routable public IPv4 address.

paid.description: True if the customer has fulfilled the invoice for this credit. False if the invoice is still outstanding. Present on credits history only.

payerEmail.description: PayPal email address of the payer.

date.description: Date and time the payment was received.

iN_tnInventory.description: Optional Filters: Select one or several of (lata,rateCenter,province)

credits.description: Full credit history, newest first. Each entry has a paid flag indicating invoice status.

subject.example: I need help with my account

email.description: Admin only: create ticket on behalf of this customer email.

campaignID.example: A 10DLC campaignID to be associated with a registered ten-digit number. eg: CONVOLUTED
An approved 10DLC campaignID assigned by The Campaign Registry. eg: CORROSIVE

portFeature.description: Per-TN feature configuration applied after the port completes.

gateway_id.description: Gateway ID to route this TN after porting.

did.description: One or more 10-digit phone numbers to port. Toll-free (8xx) numbers are separated from geographic numbers automatically.

name.description: Subscriber name exactly as it appears on the bill from the losing carrier.

lc_btn.description: Billing telephone number from the losing carrier bill.

lc_account_number.description: Account number from the losing carrier bill.

pin.description: Port-out PIN. Required by many carriers especially mobile. Contact the losing carrier to obtain.

StreetNumber.description: Street number as it appears on the service address.

StreetType.description: USPS street type abbreviation (e.g. ST, AVE, BLVD).

UnitValue.description: Optional unit designator (e.g. APT, STE) and number.

auth_person.description: Full name of the person authorized to sign the LOA.

desired_due_date.description: Optional desired port date (YYYY-MM-DD). Leave blank for standard processing time.

features.description: Optional per-TN routing, LIDB, and SMS configuration applied after the port completes.

loa_url.description: URL to download and sign the Letter of Authorization.

port_url.description: URL to track port order status.

message.example: Port request a3a2a submitted. Please complete the LOA to proceed.

get.description: Returns full account details including balance, services, and rates. Rates are read-only. Rate limited: 6 requests per hour per IP.

put.description: Updates account settings. Customers may update: timezone, notify, notify_threshold, caller_id. Administrators may additionally update: ccs, service flags, and rate fields.

post.description: Creates a new sub-account. Requires administrator credentials. The new account password is auto-generated and returned.

Returns the current API key for the account identified by username and password. No Bearer token is required or accepted — credentials must be provided in the request body. Rate limited to 6 requests per hour per IP.
Returns call detail records for a time range specified in the request body as Unix timestamps. Rate limited: 6 requests per hour per IP.

get.description: Returns the complete credit history for the account — all credits regardless of invoice status. Each entry includes a paid flag: true means the customer has fulfilled the invoice; false means the invoice is still outstanding. Use /v2.2/account/credits/paid or /v2.2/account/credits/unpaid to retrieve a filtered subset.
Returns credits where the customer has fulfilled the invoice (paid=true). These are credits that were applied to the account and have since been settled by the customer.

Returns credits that have been applied to the account but whose invoice has not yet been fulfilled by the customer (paid=false). These represent the outstanding balance the customer is expected to pay.
Returns current MRC broken down by service plus a total. Rate limited: 6 requests per hour per IP.

Returns all PayPal payments received on the account, newest first. Each entry includes the amount, date, PayPal transaction ID, payment status, and the payer email address. Payments represent real cash received; for credit adjustments see GET /v2.2/account/credits.

post.description: Submit a number port-in request. Replicates the LNP web form exactly: creates a port record, opens a support ticket, notifies the LNP team, and deducts the configured per-TN review fee from the account balance. The LNP team reviews the request and submits to the carrier - this endpoint does NOT contact the carrier directly. A signed Letter of Authorization (LOA) must be uploaded after submission. Returns 402 if the account balance is insufficient.

201.description: Port request submitted successfully. Sign the LOA at loa_url and upload proof of ownership to proceed.

400.description: Bad input - missing required field or invalid phone number.

402.description: Insufficient account balance. The configured per-TN review fee is required for each TN in the submission.

post.description: Sends a password recovery link to the email address on file. No authentication required.

get.description: Returns the current SIP URI and user agent for the registered device.

post.summary: Sign up for a new account
post.description: Creates a new account. No authentication required. Returns account credentials and API key. A verification email is sent.

get.description: Returns all IP CIDR blocks on the account IP authentication ACL.

delete.description: Removes one or more IPv4 CIDR blocks from the ACL.

409.description: One or more entries could not be removed

post.description: Adds one or more IPv4 CIDR blocks. Only routable public addresses accepted. Mask must be /8, /16, /24, or /32.

409.description: One or more entries could not be added

get.description: Returns the current authentication type, its description, and all IP ACL entries for the account.

put.description: Updates the account authentication type and/or password.

get.description: Returns all e911 records provisioned for telephone numbers on the account.

post.description: Validates a street address for e911 provisioning. Returns the validated address including an addressid for use with PUT /e911/{dn}.

put.description: Provisions an e911 address using a pre-validated addressId from POST /e911/validate.

get.description: Returns the e911 record for a specific telephone number on the account.

post.description: Validates the address and immediately provisions it to the telephone number in a single call.

get.description: Returns all system route types and user-defined SIP gateways. System routes: 1=USER, 2=T30, 3=VOICE_BRIDGE, 4=DID, 5=T38, 6=RTC_BRIDGE, 8=SDN.

post.description: Adds a SIP gateway. Must resolve to a routable public IPv4 address. Optional port as host:port. Optionally set a dial prefix and concurrent call limit.

delete.description: Removes a gateway. Will fail if DIDs are currently routed to it.

409.description: Cannot remove — DIDs still routed to it

post.description: Adds a new brand for number provisioning messaging.

409.description: Invalid Input Parameters | Invalid messagingBrandId format, should start with B and contain alphanumeric characters only

431.description: Unable to complete the request at the moment

410.description: DB Error | Error specific to order_id or customerOrderReference

post.description: Adds a new campaign for a specific messaging brand. campaignClassName and campaignStartDate are set automatically; callers do not need to provide those fields.

409.description: Invalid Input Parameters | Invalid messagingBrandId format | Invalid externalCampaignId format | externalCampaignId is not ACTIVE | externalCampaignId is not registered

431.description: Unable to complete the request at the moment

410.description: DB Error | Error specific to order_id or customerOrderReference

post.description: Check number portability. All TNs must be verified here before submitting a portInOrder — returns 412 if any TN was not pre-verified.

409.description: Invalid TN submitted | Invalid Input Parameters

200.description: Portability check successful. notPortableReason is omitted when isPortable is true.

notPortableReason.description: Only present when isPortable is false

414.description: Fields Are Required | Wireless not allowed for Customer

post.description: Submit a port-in order. All TNs must first be verified via portInAvailability — returns 412 if any TN was not pre-verified. authDate is auto-set. On success, the configured per-TN port fee is deducted from the account balance.

412.description: Pre-verification required — at least one submitted TN was not verified via portInAvailability first.

message.example: Please verify portability with portInAvailability API on all submitted tnItem(s)

200.description: Port order submitted successfully. The configured per-TN port fee is deducted from the account balance.

post.description: Update port-out PINs for phone numbers. If portOutPin is omitted for any tnItem, a random 4-digit PIN (1000-9999) is auto-generated. Only TNs belonging to the authenticated account are processed.

200.description: Success. The portOutPin object is echoed back with auto-generated PINs filled in for any tnItem that did not include one. Only TNs belonging to the authenticated account are included.

portOutPin.description: Auto-generated 4-digit PIN if not provided in request
Echo of the submitted portOutPin object with auto-generated PINs filled in

post.description: Get port status summary for the authenticated account, ordered by timestamp descending.

portStatusSummary.description: Port status records, ordered by timestamp descending

post.description: Optional Filters: Select one or several of (lata,rateCenter,province).

Optional countBy: Select one of (state,rateCenter,npaNxx,block) default is (npaNxx). Optional Filters: Select one of (npa,lata,rcAbbre,locState). Results are filtered to items matching the submitted filter field.

200.description: Success. tnInventoryCoverageList is filtered to items matching the submitted filter field (npa, lata, rcAbbre, or locState).

tnInventoryCoverageList.description: Filtered by whichever of npa, lata, rcAbbre, or locState was provided in the request

post.description: Assigns a campaign to a list of telephone numbers for messaging. Each tn must be 10 digits and each tnFeature.messaging.tnCampaign must be a 7-character string starting with "C". customerOrderReference is auto-set if omitted.

431.description: Unable to complete the request at the moment

409.description: Invalid TN Submitted | Invalid Input Parameters

post.description: Order numbers and optionally route numbers. Route can be obtained from Gateways list API.

orderId.description: Timestamp-based order reference (not the upstream IQ order ID)

400.description: Bad input — tn must be an array of tnItem.

message.example: tn should be an array of tnItem

get.description: Returns the Caller ID Name registered for the given 10-digit telephone number. Billed per lookup. Requires CNAM service to be enabled.

402.description: Service not enabled or insufficient balance

get.description: Returns the Location Routing Number (LRN) and carrier details for the destination number. Billed per lookup.

200.description: Campaign status returned for all submitted IDs

post.description: Query 10DLC CampaignID(s) assigned by The Campaign Registry.

200.description: Messaging disabled on all submitted numbers

numbers_enabled.description: Numbers successfully disabled, keyed by TN

409.description: Partial failure — one or more numbers not found on account or already disabled

post.description: Disable SMS and MMS for numbers
Get delivery reports

200.description: Messaging enabled on all submitted numbers

409.description: Partial failure — one or more numbers could not be enabled

post.description: Enable SMS and MMS for numbers. An approved 10DLC campaignID will soon be required for enablement.
Get MMS Message Detail Report
Get SMS Message Detail Report

400.description: Message rejected — upstream messaging carrier returned success=false. success and result.referenceId are stripped; result.transactionGUID is added.

401.description: Unauthorized — source number not on account

405.description: MMS not enabled on account or number

406.description: STOP Blocked — destination has opted out

post.description: Send MMS (Multimedia Messaging Service)

409.description: Partial failure — one or more numbers could not be updated

post.description: Set routing for incoming messages

409.description: Partial failure — one or more numbers could not be updated

post.description: Set routing for outgoing messages

401.description: Unauthorized — source number not on account

405.description: SMS not enabled on account or number

406.description: STOP Blocked — destination has opted out

post.description: Send SMS (Short Message Service)

409.description: One or more numbers not found on account

post.description: Get SMS and MMS status for numbers

get.description: Returns all telephone numbers on the account with full detail including route, gateway, forwarding, CNAM, SMS, and fax configuration.

post.description: Assigns a telephone number to the account. Account balance must cover the monthly DID rate.

delete.description: Permanently removes the number from the account. Numbers currently porting cannot be removed.

put.summary: Enable or disable inbound CNAM lookup

put.description: Sets the outbound Caller ID Name (CNAM/LIDB) for the number. Writes the name to the local cache (used by the softswitch for outbound caller-id) and then submits the update to the upstream LIDB carrier. The local cache update is durable; a carrier-side failure is reported via the response's carrierStatus field but does not fail the request. Max 15 alphanumeric characters.

delete.summary: Release a number back to the pool
delete.description: Removes the number from the account and returns it to the available pool. Numbers currently porting cannot be released.

put.description: Sets the gateway route for a number.
Sets or updates SMS routing for the number.

get.description: Returns the SMS routing configuration for the number. Type is one of: email, webhook, sip.

put.description: Sets the inbound DNIS translation for the number.

get.description: Returns support tickets. Customers see only their own tickets. Administrators see all tickets.

post.description: Creates a new support ticket. The customer email is automatically populated from the authenticated account. Administrators may optionally specify a different email.

put.description: Updates the status of a ticket. Customers can only update their own tickets.

get.description: Returns a ticket including its threads. Customers can only view their own tickets.

post.summary: Add a reply to a ticket
post.description: Adds a customer reply to an existing ticket. Customers can only reply to their own tickets.

get.description: Returns all message threads for a ticket. Customers can only view their own tickets.

tags.description: Account profile, settings, billing charges, credit history, call detail records, SIP registration status, and self-service signup and password recovery.
tags.name: Account

tags.description: Manage the IP Access Control List for IP-based SIP authentication. Only routable public IPv4 CIDRs with masks /8, /16, /24, or /32 are accepted.
tags.name: ACL

tags.description: Manage the account SIP authentication type (Digest, IP Auth, or both) and password.
tags.name: Authentication

tags.description: Provision and manage e911 emergency service records for telephone numbers on the account.
tags.name: e911

tags.description: Manage SIP gateways (outbound trunks). System route types (USER, T30, VOICE_BRIDGE, DID, T38, RTC_BRIDGE, SDN) are always present and read-only.
tags.name: Gateways

tags.description: Number ordering, search, porting, and messaging campaign provisioning.
tags.name: iNumbering

tags.description: Per-lookup billed services. CNAM returns the registered Caller ID Name. LRN returns Location Routing Number and carrier details. Requires the service to be enabled on the account.
tags.name: Lookups
Messaging
tags.description: SMS/MMS sending, routing, messaging campaigns, and delivery records.
Manage telephone numbers: routing, forwarding, DNIS translation, inbound CNAM lookup, outbound Caller ID Name (LIDB), SMS routing, and fax-to-email.
tags.name: Numbers

tags.description: Support ticket management. Customers see only their own tickets. Administrators see all tickets.
tags.name: Support

info.description: VoiceTel REST API. All resource identifiers are in the URL path. Authenticate with your API key as a Bearer token. Rate-limited endpoints: account/info, account/mrc, account/cdr — 6 requests per hour per IP.
info.title: VoiceTel API v2.2
